name: Irkutsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the rivers (Lena, Angara) and Lake Baikal, plus the eternal sky
purity, and the snow of the long Siberian winter
the babr (Siberian tiger) of the historic coat of arms
the sable carried by the babr
the cedar branches that flank the central emblem

description: A white central field flanked by two blue vertical stripes (a slightly narrower one at the hoist), charged in the centre with a black running babr carrying a red sable in its jaws between two green cedar branches.
history: Adopted on 16 July 1997 by the Legislative Assembly of Irkutsk Oblast. The babr-with-sable charge is taken from the historic coat of arms of Irkutsk dating to the 17th century.

features.symbolism: the blue panels at hoist and fly recall the great waters of the Lena, Angara and Lake Baikal as well as the sky; the central white field bears the historic emblem of the Irkutsk lands.

features.role: babr with a sable in its mouth
features.symbolism: a black babr (a heraldic Siberian tiger) running and carrying a red sable in its jaws; the historic emblem of Irkutsk, recorded in the 17th century to symbolise wealth from the fur trade.
two green cedar branches flanking the babr; symbolise the rich Siberian taiga.
